interpretation: Population posterior mean μ_α(T-,T+) = +1.512 → α(T-,T+) = exp(1.512) ≈ 4.54 (canonical Zhang: 3.0)
Population posterior mean μ_α(T-,TP) = +1.712 → α(T-,TP) = exp(1.712) ≈ 5.54 (canonical Zhang: 4.0)
Bruchovsky cohort suggests T- inhibition coefficients ~50% higher than Zhang's nominal values

comparison_to_default_prior.verdict: Hier-informed prior produced WORSE R-hat than default. Tightening marginals does not constrain the joint anti-correlated direction between α(T-,T+) and α(T-,TP).

interpretation: Diagonal hier-informed prior is INSUFFICIENT to fix per-patient NUTS convergence on the rank-deficient posterior.
The unidentifiable direction is a joint anti-correlation between α(T-,T+) and α(T-,TP) (§3.3); tightening each marginal independently does not constrain motion along this 1D ridge.
Next Phase 4 task: non-diagonal prior with proper off-diagonal covariance structure (e.g., FIM-inverse-based covariance or full hierarchical-posterior empirical covariance).

WP1 claims unaffected: PE-vs-PA disagreement depends on rank-deficient direction width, not on per-patient mode-finding. The hierarchical pooling in §6.11 DOES converge cleanly and confirms the population-level posterior shape.
